column1: # Review log for cited sources — the unified resolution log.
# Add a row when:
#   1. You ADD a new entry to src/data/sources.ts.
#   2. You RESOLVE an audit issue (audit:link or audit:report).
#   3. You DO A PERIODIC REVIEW and verify a citation still backs the claim.
# The audit makes the level of human involvement transparent rather than
# absent: AI assistance is allowed and useful, especially during active
# development; the row's `kind` column records honestly what kind of
# review happened.
# Format: id \t YYYY-MM-DD \t reviewer \t kind \t notes (tab-separated)
# Lines starting with # are comments.
# `id` is the stable source slug from src/data/sources.ts — the outer key
# in SOURCES (e.g. `kff-employer-health-benefits`) or a synthesized
# `state-${kind}-${code}` for the per-state agency maps (e.g.
# `state-dor-ca`, `state-snap-tx`). Keying by id rather than URL means
# review history follows the source through URL changes.
# `kind` records what kind of verification happened:
#   - `human` — eyes-on-source, no AI assistance. Use this only when you
#               personally read the destination and confirmed the cited
#               figures. Required for community-submitted reviews via the
#               audit:report issue template.
#   - `ai`    — AI was involved in proposing, extracting, or refreshing
#               this entry. Whatever human review followed (a glance, a
#               careful read, none) is self-reported and unverifiable, so
#               the kind doesn't try to subdivide. Be honest: if you
#               weren't eyes-on-source, it's `ai`.
# An earlier three-state vocabulary (`ai-assisted`, `ai-proposed`) is
# still accepted by the parser for backwards compatibility — both fold
# into `ai` at parse time. New rows should use `ai`.
# Reviewer should be your GitHub username (no @ prefix). Renders as a
# profile link in audit/links/status.md.
# Legacy rows (pre-kind column, 4 fields) parse as kind=`human`.
# id
hud-handbook-4350-3
column4: HUD Handbook 4350.3 REV-1, Change 4 (Nov 2013). Confirmed the landing page lists the handbook + chapters; Ch. 3 covers occupancy. Replaces the 404'd /sites/dfiles/OCHCO/documents/4350.3.pdf citation.

column1: kff-employer-health-benefits
column4: KFF moved the annual survey to a year-stable series archive. Confirmed the page links to the latest (2025) report with the figures we anchor employer health-insurance employee-share to. Replaces the 404'd /health-costs/report/employer-health-benefits-annual-survey/.

column1: care-com-childcare
column4: Care.com renamed the cost-of-care landing page. Confirmed the new URL hosts the current Cost of Care Report with per-metro childcare cost figures. Replaces the 404'd /c/cost-of-childcare/.

column1: child-care-aware
column4: Child Care Aware retired the /state-fact-sheets/ page. The 2024 Price & Supply report at /price-landscape24/ has an interactive state selector with per-state child-care price breakdowns — same content territory we cited the fact sheets for. Year-stamped slug; staleness audit will surface it on the next cycle.
